meta.title: Stripe Error Code Reference
meta.description: Structured reference of Stripe API error codes, decline codes, and handling strategies.

error_types.card_error: A card-level problem prevented the charge. The error code and decline code provide specific details.
error_types.request_error: Something was wrong with the API request itself (invalid parameters, authentication, idempotency).
error_types.api_error: An unexpected error on Stripe's end. Rare. Retry with exponential backoff.
error_types.idempotency_error: Two requests used the same idempotency key but had different parameters.
error_types.invalid_request_error: Invalid parameters were supplied to Stripe's API.

errors.cause: The card was declined by the issuing bank. The decline_code field provides more specific detail.
errors.fix: Check the decline_code for specifics. Do not retry without user action. Ask the customer to contact their bank or use a different card. Never share the raw decline_code with the user.

errors.cause: The card does not have enough funds to complete the purchase.
errors.fix: Ask the customer to use a different card or add funds. Do not retry the same card.

errors.cause: The card has been reported lost by the cardholder.
errors.fix: Do not reveal this reason to avoid tipping off fraudsters. Show generic decline message. Flag the transaction for review.

errors.cause: The card has been reported stolen by the cardholder.
errors.fix: Do not reveal the specific reason. Flag for fraud review. Block the user session if warranted. Show generic decline message.

errors.user_facing_message: Your card was declined. Please contact your bank.
errors.cause: Generic decline from the issuing bank with no specific reason provided.
errors.fix: Ask the customer to contact their bank. They may need to whitelist the merchant or authorize the transaction. Retry after bank contact.

errors.user_facing_message: This transaction type is not allowed on this card.
errors.cause: The transaction type is not permitted for this card. Common with prepaid cards trying to pay for subscriptions, or corporate cards with merchant category restrictions.
errors.fix: Ask the customer to use a different card. Debit or prepaid cards often have restrictions on recurring billing.

errors.cause: The card has exceeded its spending limit for a given time period, as configured by the issuing bank.
errors.fix: Ask the customer to contact their bank to increase limits or use a different card. Implement retry backoff if retrying failed subscription charges.

errors.cause: The card's expiration date is in the past.
errors.fix: Prompt the customer to update their card details. Use Stripe's hosted payment UI (Checkout, Payment Element) which validates expiry client-side before submission.

errors.user_facing_message: Your card's security code is incorrect.
errors.cause: The CVC/CVV number provided does not match the card.
errors.fix: Ask the customer to re-enter the CVC. After multiple incorrect attempts, the card may be blocked by the bank.

errors.cause: The card number fails Luhn algorithm validation or does not match a known card number format.
errors.fix: Validate card numbers client-side using Stripe.js or a Luhn check before submission to avoid unnecessary API calls.

errors.user_facing_message: Your card's expiration month is invalid.
errors.cause: The expiry month is not a valid value (1-12).
errors.fix: Validate expiry input client-side. Stripe.js handles this automatically when using the Payment Element.

errors.user_facing_message: Your card's expiration year is invalid.
errors.cause: The expiry year is in the past or not a valid year.
errors.fix: Validate expiry year client-side. Prefer 2-digit year input that auto-converts to the nearest valid year.

errors.cause: The payment requires 3D Secure (SCA) authentication and the charge was attempted without it, or the customer did not complete 3DS.
errors.fix: Use PaymentIntents with `stripe.confirmCardPayment()` to handle 3DS automatically. Check `payment_intent.next_action` for redirect details. Never charge cards directly in SCA-required regions.

errors.user_facing_message: An error occurred processing your payment.
errors.cause: The API key used is invalid, revoked, or a test key is being used in live mode (or vice versa).
errors.fix: Verify the API key in your Stripe Dashboard. Ensure you are using the correct key for the environment (test vs live). Never expose secret keys in client-side code.

errors.cause: No Stripe account was found matching the provided API key or connect account ID.
errors.fix: Verify the Stripe-Account header (for Connect) contains a valid connected account ID. Check that the account has not been deactivated.

errors.user_facing_message: An error occurred. Please try again.
errors.cause: Too many requests made to the Stripe API in too short a time.
errors.fix: Implement exponential backoff with jitter. Start with 1s, double each retry, up to 8 retries. Check your API usage in the Stripe Dashboard. Batch operations where possible.

errors.cause: The resource (customer, payment method, invoice, subscription) does not exist or does not belong to the current API key's account.
errors.fix: Verify the resource ID is correct and exists in the right Stripe account. Test vs live mode resources are separate. Log the resource ID for debugging.

errors.cause: A required parameter was not included in the API request.
errors.fix: Check the Stripe API docs for required parameters. The error message will name the missing parameter. Enable Stripe request logging in development mode.

errors.cause: A parameter was passed as an empty string where a non-empty value is required.
errors.fix: Validate all parameters before sending to the API. Do not pass empty strings for required fields; omit them entirely or pass null.

errors.user_facing_message: An error occurred. Please try again.
errors.cause: A request is still in progress for this idempotency key. Concurrent requests with the same idempotency key are not allowed.
errors.fix: Wait for the in-flight request to complete before retrying with the same idempotency key. Implement proper retry logic that respects in-progress requests.

errors.cause: Two requests used the same idempotency key but with different parameters — the key was not unique enough or was accidentally reused.
errors.fix: Generate idempotency keys as UUIDs tied to the specific action (e.g. order ID + attempt number). Never reuse keys across different operations.

errors.cause: An automatic subscription renewal requires 3DS authentication. This happens for SCA-mandated regions (EEA, UK) when the renewal cannot use off-session exemptions.
errors.fix: Use `payment_intent.client_secret` from the invoice and redirect the customer to complete 3DS. Implement the subscription incomplete webhook flow with `invoice.payment_action_required` event.

errors.cause: Attempting to retrieve an upcoming invoice for a customer with no active subscription.
errors.fix: Check whether the customer has an active subscription before calling `stripe.invoices.retrieveUpcoming()`. Handle the 404 as an expected case.

errors.cause: Stripe Tax cannot determine the customer's tax location because the address is incomplete, invalid, or conflicts between the shipping and billing address.
errors.fix: Collect complete customer addresses (line1, city, postal_code, country). Validate addresses before sending to Stripe. Check Stripe Tax configuration in the Dashboard.

errors.cause: Attempting to capture a charge that has already been captured.
errors.fix: Check charge status before attempting capture. Use `charge.captured` to verify state. Implement idempotency to prevent double-capture in retry scenarios.

errors.cause: Attempting to refund a charge that has already been fully refunded.
errors.fix: Check `charge.refunded` and `charge.amount_refunded` before issuing a refund. Track refund state in your database to prevent duplicate refund attempts.

errors.cause: The charge has an active dispute and certain operations (like refunding) are blocked during the dispute process.
errors.fix: Resolve the dispute first. Submit evidence via the Stripe Dashboard or API. Once the dispute is resolved, you can proceed with other operations on the charge.

errors.cause: Attempting to create a payout larger than the available Stripe balance.
errors.fix: Check balance before initiating payouts: `stripe.balance.retrieve()`. Set up automatic payouts in the Dashboard to avoid manual balance management. Account for payout timing differences.

errors.cause: The ACH debit was declined by the bank. Reasons include closed account, insufficient funds, unauthorized debit, or frozen account.
errors.fix: Contact the customer to verify bank account details. Use Stripe's bank account verification (micro-deposits or Plaid instant verification) before charging. Handle `payment_intent.payment_failed` webhooks.

errors.cause: The webhook signature cannot be verified. The raw body was modified before verification, the wrong signing secret was used, or the timestamp is outside the tolerance window.
errors.fix: Use the raw request body for signature verification, not a parsed JSON body. Use `stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, sig, secret)`. Verify you are using the signing secret for the correct endpoint (test vs live, different endpoints have different secrets).

errors.cause: The connected account ID in the Stripe-Account header is invalid, deactivated, or does not belong to your platform.
errors.fix: Verify the connected account exists and is active: `stripe.accounts.retrieve(accountId)`. Handle account deactivation by blocking payouts to inactive accounts.

errors.cause: The address provided for a Connect account does not match the country of the account.
errors.fix: Ensure the address country matches the account's country. Validate address fields for the specific country's format (postal code format varies by country).

errors.cause: The platform API key has expired. This occurs with restricted keys that have expiry set.
errors.fix: Rotate the API key in the Stripe Dashboard and update all services using it. Implement key rotation monitoring to catch expiry before it impacts users.

errors.user_facing_message: An error occurred. Please try again.
errors.cause: An operation was attempted on a PaymentIntent in an incompatible state. E.g. confirming a PaymentIntent that has already succeeded or been cancelled.
errors.fix: Check `payment_intent.status` before performing operations. States: `requires_payment_method`, `requires_confirmation`, `requires_action`, `processing`, `succeeded`, `canceled`.

errors.user_facing_message: This payment method is not supported.
errors.cause: The payment method type is not included in the PaymentIntent's `payment_method_types` list.
errors.fix: Update the PaymentIntent to include the desired payment method type, or create a new one with the correct types. Consider using `automatic_payment_methods: { enabled: true }` to support all Stripe-enabled methods.

errors.cause: The coupon being applied has passed its `redeem_by` date or has exceeded its `max_redemptions` count.
errors.fix: Check coupon validity before applying: `stripe.coupons.retrieve(couponId)`. Handle the expired state gracefully in the checkout flow.

errors.cause: The Price or Plan ID referenced in a subscription does not exist or has been archived.
errors.fix: Use the Prices API (not the deprecated Plans API). Verify the price ID in the Dashboard. Never hardcode price IDs; store them in config/env vars.

errors.user_facing_message: A network error occurred. Please try again.
errors.cause: Could not connect to Stripe's servers. Usually a transient network issue, DNS failure, or firewall blocking outbound connections to api.stripe.com.
errors.fix: Implement retry with exponential backoff. Check outbound firewall rules for api.stripe.com on port 443. Monitor Stripe status at status.stripe.com.

errors.user_facing_message: An error occurred. Please try again.
errors.cause: An unexpected error on Stripe's end. These are rare.
errors.fix: Retry with exponential backoff (up to 3-5 times). If the error persists, check status.stripe.com. Log the `request_id` from the response header and include it in any support requests.

errors.cause: The currency specified is not supported by the payment method or the Stripe account's country.
errors.fix: Check supported currencies for the payment method type in Stripe docs. Verify the account's settlement currency. Use `presentment_currency` to show the customer's local currency while settling in a supported currency.

errors.user_facing_message: The payment amount is too small.
errors.cause: The charge amount is below the minimum allowed for the currency (e.g. $0.50 USD, $0.30 EUR, 50 JPY).
errors.fix: Ensure amounts meet the minimum per-currency threshold. Check minimums in Stripe docs. Consider consolidating small charges into a single larger charge.

errors.user_facing_message: The payment amount is too large.
errors.cause: The charge amount exceeds the maximum allowed for the currency.
errors.fix: Check maximum charge limits per currency in Stripe docs. For very large transactions, split into multiple charges or use wire transfers.

errors.cause: The email provided for customer creation is not a valid email format.
errors.fix: Validate email addresses client-side before sending to the API. Use a regex or email validation library. The `email` field on Customer is optional but must be valid if provided.

errors.cause: A real card number was used in test mode. Stripe blocks real card numbers in test environments.
errors.fix: Use Stripe's test card numbers (e.g. 4242 4242 4242 4242 for Visa, 4000 0025 6000 0006 for 3DS). See the full list in Stripe's testing documentation.

errors.user_facing_message: This payment method is not available.
errors.cause: The payment method (SEPA, iDEAL, Klarna, etc.) is not enabled for the Stripe account or not available in the customer's region.
errors.fix: Enable the payment method in Stripe Dashboard under Settings > Payment methods. Verify the account's country supports the method. Use `payment_method_types` to restrict what is shown.

errors.cause: An operation was attempted on a SetupIntent in an incompatible state.
errors.fix: Check `setup_intent.status` before operations. Valid states: `requires_payment_method`, `requires_confirmation`, `requires_action`, `processing`, `succeeded`, `canceled`.

errors.cause: The mandate associated with a SEPA or ACH payment method has been revoked or expired.
errors.fix: Collect a new mandate from the customer using a SetupIntent. Check mandate status via the Mandate object. Handle `mandate.updated` webhooks to track mandate state changes.

errors.user_facing_message: The linked bank account is no longer active.
errors.cause: The bank account linked via Stripe Financial Connections has become inactive, closed, or the access token has been revoked by the user.
errors.fix: Prompt the user to re-link their bank account. Handle `financial_connections.account.disconnected` webhooks. Check account status before initiating transfers.

errors.cause: The source transaction used for a Connect transfer does not have sufficient funds available.
errors.fix: Verify the charge has been captured and is not in a disputed state before transferring. Account for Stripe fees when calculating the transferable amount.

errors.cause: The tax ID number provided does not match the expected format for the given type and country.
errors.fix: Validate tax ID format client-side before submission. Different types (VAT, EIN, ABN) have different formats. The error message will indicate the expected format.
